name: Body Mass Index (BMI)
description: Body Mass Index is a value derived from the mass and height of a person. It is used as a screening tool to identify possible weight problems for adults.
interpretation: A BMI <18.5 is considered underweight. 18.5-24.9 is normal. 25.0-29.9 is overweight. ≥30.0 is classified as obese. Note that BMI does not directly measure body fat and may be misleading in highly muscular individuals or the elderly.
warnings: Pediatric BMI must be plotted on age- and sex-specific percentiles.
references: 1. Clinical Guidelines on the Identification, Evaluation, and Treatment of Overweight and Obesity in Adults. NIH Publication No. 98-4083. 1998.
examples: A 30-year-old male with a height of 1.80 m and weight of 85 kg has a BMI of 26.2 kg/m², falling into the overweight category.
notes: Calculated as weight in kilograms divided by the square of height in meters.
